question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be between $102,000 and $104,000 on November 13?

Description: This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

evidences.title: A Quick Liquidity Update | Binance Blog
evidences.content: Image 1: Binance Buy Crypto Markets Trade Futures Earn Square More Log InSign Up Image 2 Scan to Download App iOS & Android More Download Options BlogMarkets Zero BTC Fees: A Quick Liquidity Update # Zero BTC Fees: A Quick Liquidity Update 2022-09-29 ## Main Takeaways Binance continues to offer zero-fee trading on a selection of the most popular BTC trading pairs on its spot exchange. Binance’s BTC/USDT pair has improved its market depth by roughly 50% since the beginning of this promotion and now has improved liquidity. Trading fees aren’t the only costs when using an exchange, and users must be aware of the hidden costs arising from poor liquidity on order books. [...] If we now take a look at the BTC/USDT pair (which accounts for 70% of trading volume across the thirteen pairs available at the time), there has been an improvement of 53% since the campaign’s start. For anyone trading BTC for USDT and vice versa, this can mean faster filling of orders and a reduction in price slippage compared to before the fee removal. _Comparing the daily average from 10 June - 7 July (4 weeks) before the fee removal’s effective date and from 9 July - 9 Sep (10 weeks) after the effective date...

evidences.title: Bitcoin USD Price: Quote, Market Cap, Forecast & News (BTCUSD)
evidences.content: Bitcoin is trading essentially flat, hovering near $63,100 after a volatile session that saw an intraday high near $64,200 before sellers pushed back. The week's narrative has been driven by a weak June jobs report (57,000 vs. 113,000 forecast) that triggered a short squeeze and roughly $450M in short liquidations, but today's mood turned cautious after Strategy (MSTR) disclosed it sold 3,588 BTC (~$216M) to fund preferred stock dividends — its largest operational Bitcoin sale since 2022. Bernstein analysts noted Strategy has been 'a balancing force' limiting deeper selloffs, making the disclosure a sentiment shock. Meanwhile, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs snapped a 10-day outflow streak with $221M in inflows, whale accumulation of $16.7B over two weeks signals structural support near $60K, and [...] 0.14% Bitcoin is trading essentially flat near $64,000, holding above key support after a sharp intraday swing from $63,784 to $65,469. The primary narrative centers on a meaningful deceleration in spot ETF outflows — weekly redemptions fell roughly 87% from early June peaks — signaling the heaviest phase of institutional selling may be passing. Strategy added another 520 BTC for ~$35 mill...

evidences.title: Types of Fees on Binance (Detailed Summary)
evidences.content: Taker: The person who executes an order immediately, usually market orders. When you take liquidity from the market, you are a taker. 2.2 How to calculate Spot trading fees Trading fees on Binance depend on your account level. These levels are determined based on the trading volume over the last 30 days and the BNB balance you hold. Binance has a fee reduction program when you use BNB to pay trading fees. Basic trading fee (Level 0): Maker: 0.10% Taker: 0.10% Example: If you buy 1 BTC for $25,000, the trading fee will be: Maker: 0.10% 25,000 = 25 USD Taker: 0.10% 25,000 = 25 USD 2.3 Fee reduction when using BNB [...] 2.3 Fee reduction when using BNB If you use BNB to pay trading fees, you will receive a discount. Currently, Binance offers a 25% discount for Spot transactions when using BNB. Example: Taker fee when using BNB will be 0.10% (1 - 25%) = 0.075%. If you buy 1 BTC for $25,000 and use BNB for payment, the fee will be 0.075% 25,000 = 18.75 USD. 2.4 VIP levels Binance has a VIP level system from VIP 0 to VIP 9, based on: Trading volume over the last 30 days (measured in BTC). BNB balance. The higher the level, the lower the trading fees. Image 3 VIP fee on Binance ### 3....

evidences.title: Seasonality in Bitcoin: Examining Almost A Decade of Price Data
evidences.content: The largest 1-hour/4-hour returns on average are during the 12:00–16:00 session on Fridays. Average returns for the 4-hour timeframe are lowest in the early hours of Sunday, Tuesday and Friday between midnight and 4am, as well as 16:00–20:00 on Thursdays and Fridays. Hourly volatility has been greatest during Wednesday and Thursday afternoon (15:00–17:00) according to the data. The largest average 4-hour volatility is found during Friday’s 12:00–16:00 trading session, as well as midday onwards for Wednesday and Thursday. BTC-USD has been least volatile on Saturday and Sunday mornings (04:00–08:00) and evenings (18:00–19:00). [...] On average, the biggest percentage increases during the 12:00–16:00 session on Friday. ### Average Volatility (4-hour) for Bitcoin Press enter or click to view image in full size Image 18 Source: CryptoDatum. Volatility is usually highest in the 12:00–16:00 session on Fridays and Wednesdays, as well as all sessions in the second half of Thursday. On average, volatility is lowest in the hours of 00:00–08:00 Sunday morning and 04:00–08:00 Saturday morning. ### Average Candle Body Ratio (4-hour) For Bitcoin Press enter or click to view image in full size...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price Phases: Navigating Bitcoin's Volatility Trends
evidences.content: ### Reversal Phase The Reversal Phase marks the beginning of a new cycle, characterized by bitcoin’s price rapidly declining from the previous cycle’s highs. Defined by high volatility and a low percentage of addresses in profit, this phase signifies the abrupt onset of a bear market and is often a challenging time for bitcoin holders. The Reversal Phase is followed by the Bottoming Phase. ### Bottoming Phase The Bottoming Phase represents a quiet period following the turbulence of the Reversal Phase. It begins with a shift from high to low volatility, alongside the continuation of a low percentage of addresses in profit. [...] Understanding bitcoin price phases begins with defining when profit and volatility are considered high or low. For this analysis, the percentage of addresses in profit is classified as high when it exceeds 95% and low when it falls below this threshold. Alternatively, high and low volatility can be measured using percentiles, allowing for more accurate comparisons over time. This analysis applies the fifth percentile of all one-year realized volatility data from July 19, 2010, to February 4, 2025. Bitcoin’s realized volatility is classified as low on any...

evidences.title: Bayesian Analysis of Bitcoin Volatility Using Minute-by ... - MDPI
evidences.content: This study analyzes the volatility of Bitcoin using stochastic volatility models fitted to one-minute transaction data for the BTC/USDT pair between 1 April 2023, and 31 March 2024. Bernstein polynomial terms were introduced to accommodate intraday and intraweek seasonality, and flexible return distributions were used to capture distributional characteristics. Seven return distributions—normal, Student-t, skew-t, Laplace, asymmetric Laplace (AL), variance gamma, and skew variance gamma—were considered. We further incorporated explanatory variables derived from the trading volume and price changes to assess the effects of order flow. Our results reveal structural market changes, including a clear regime shift around October 2023, when the asymmetric Laplace distribution became the dominant [...] Electronic stock trading has recently generated large volumes of granular data, such as trade sizes. The availability of such data has shifted the focus of volatility analysis from interday to intraday frequencies. More specifically, volatility is now analyzed at intervals of one to five minutes . The trading of financial instruments follows characteristic patterns throughout the day, a p...
